title: Mental health factors key to recovery in older adults with hip fractures
summary: New research to identify factors associated with improved outcomes for older adults recovering from hip fractures found a connection with mental health and psychological resilience.

title: ‘Groundbreaking’ potential cure for sickle cell in England approved for NHS use
summary: Clinical trials find one-time gene therapy exa-cel offers ‘functional cure’ in 96.6% of patients

title: Appendicitis After Initiation of Mounjaro (Tirzepatide)
summary: Novel case of a 73 year old female, without typical risk factors or obvious triggers, who developed appendicitis one week following the initiation of Mounjaro.

title: Australia invests $6.8m in building health and climate research capacity
summary: The Australian government is investing nearly $6.8m (A$11m) to build research capacity on the health impacts of climate change.

title: Social Anxiety disorder in The Visually Impaired Versus Healthy Contro
summary: Comparing self-esteem and social anxiety disorder (SAD) levels in visually impaired against sighted individuals, and exploring SAD with sociodemographic factors

title: WHO Chief Pats Guinea For Fighting Out Sleeping Sickness
summary: Symptoms include fever, headaches, joint pain, and, in advanced stages, neurological symptoms like confusion, disrupted sleep patterns, and behavioural changes

title: Frequent social engagement may delay dementia onset
summary: Visiting friends, attending parties and going to church may help keep your brain healthy, according to research conducted at Rush.

title: Trial for blood tests to diagnose dementia accepts first participants
summary: A country-wide trial aiming to revolutionise how dementia is diagnosed using blood tests is now underway

title: Antibody therapy provides protection against severe H5N1 avian flu in monkeys
summary: A prophylactic antibody-based immune therapy protects monkeys against severe disease caused by H5N1 avian flu, University of Pittsburgh and NIH Vaccine Research Center researchers report today in Science.

title: Scratching found to both aggravate inflammation and boost immune defenses
summary: New research uncovers the dual nature of scratching an itch; although it can worsen skin inflammation, it can also boost immune defenses against bacterial infections at the injury site.
